Description: A physical thing made or modified by humans, e.g. a painting, sculpture, or manuscript
A born-digital or digitised resource existing only in computers, e.g. a web page or digital image
An individual human being, e.g. Rembrandt
A collective of people capable of acting together, e.g. a museum department or workshop
A geographic location, typically with coordinates, e.g. Paris
A category, concept, or vocabulary term, e.g. painting or sculpting
A human language, e.g. English or Dutch
A physical material or substance, e.g. oil paint or marble
A unit of measurement, e.g. centimetres or inches
A monetary currency, e.g. USD or EUR
Conceptual image/visual content shown by a physical or digital object, e.g. the Mona Lisa image
Textual content carried by a physical or digital object, e.g. the text of a book
An entirely abstract work that is neither linguistic nor visual, e.g. the notion of an exhibition
A set or collection of entities, e.g. a museum collection
An activity carried out by people or groups, e.g. an exhibition or provenance transfer
A historical period or era, e.g. the Renaissance
An event that may be depicted or referenced, e.g. a battle
The making of a physical object
The creation of a conceptual work (text / image / concept / set)
The destruction of a physical object
The erasure or deletion of a digital object
The birth of a person
The death of a person
The formation of a group or organization
The dissolution of a group or organization
The acquisition of ownership of an object
The transfer of physical custody of an object without ownership change
The physical movement of an object to a new location
A significant change or modification to an object
Adding a part to an object
Removing a part from an object
Recording a previously held belief or assertion
A person joining a group
A person leaving a group
An encounter with an object, e.g. archaeological find
A payment of money
A right held over an object (appropriated usage in Linked Art)
A name or appellation for an entity
A formal identifier such as an accession number
A span of time with begin/end dates
A measured dimension such as height, width, weight, or duration
An amount of money
A reference to an external standard or document
